The triploid endosperm is crucial as it provides nourishment to the developing embryo. The other options are incorrect: the pollen tube does not directly affect post-fertilization changes, antipodal cells typically degenerate instead of doubling, and synergids do not fuse with the egg cell to form a zygote.

options.text: polar nuclei will form triploid endosperm.
options.explanation: After double fertilization, one sperm cell fuses with two polar nuclei to form a triploid endosperm, which nourishes the developing embryo.

options.text: egg cell will fuse with synergids to form diploid zygote.
options.explanation: The egg cell fuses with a sperm cell to form a diploid zygote, not with a synergid.

questions.explanation: In angiosperms, double fertilization involves two key events: one sperm cell fuses with the egg cell to form a diploid zygote, and another sperm cell fuses with two polar nuclei to form a triploid endosperm. The triploid endosperm is crucial as it provides nourishment to the developing embryo. The other options are incorrect: the pollen tube does not directly affect post-fertilization changes, antipodal cells typically degenerate instead of doubling, and synergids do not fuse with the egg cell to form a zygote.

questions.text: The given diageam shows the life cycle of a plant.The feature in the given life cycle due to which this plant is classified as a tracheophyte is thepresence of:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: While haploid spores are part of many plant life cycles, they are not unique to tracheophytes.
Tracheophytes are characterized by a dominant sporophyte phase in their life cycle, that is why this option is correct.
An independent gametophyte is more characteristic of bryophytes rather than tracheophytes.
Antheridium and archegonium are structures found in many plants, including non-tracheophytes, so they do not specifically classify a plant as a tracheophyte.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is the presence of a dominant sporophyte.In tracheophytes, the sporophyte is the prominent and visible phase of the life cycle.

questions.text: In liverworts, asexual reproduction takes place by:
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: Fragmentation of thalli and gemmae cups
options.explanation: This is correct option. Liverworts reproduce asexually through the fragmentation of thalli and the formation of gemmae cups.
This is incorrect. While thalli fragmentation is a method of asexual reproduction, oospores are related to sexual reproduction, not asexual.
This is incorrect. Gemmae cups are involved in liverwort asexual reproduction, but zoospores are not a reproductive structure associated with liverworts.
This is incorrect. Both oospores and zoospores are related to sexual reproduction or different organisms, not the asexual reproduction of liverworts.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is fragmentation of thalli and gemmae cups. Liverworts can reproduce asexually by breaking off parts of their thalli, which can then grow into new plants. Additionally, gemmae cups are specialized structures that produce gemmae, small reproductive units that can develop into new individuals.

questions.text: In club mosses (lycopsida), microphylls are evolved from:
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: A short stem bearing side branches
options.explanation: This suggests microphylls evolved from a central stem with branches, but it does not account for the specific structural formation of microphylls.

options.text: Small projecting extensions of stem tissue
options.explanation: This implies microphylls are simple protrusions, which doesn't accurately describe the evolutionary infilling process.

options.text: Side branches dividing in the same spatial plane
options.explanation: While branches dividing in the same plane can form a leaf-like structure, it doesn't explain the development of microphylls specifically.

options.text: Infilling of simple tissues between lateral branches
options.explanation: This describes the correct evolutionary process where microphylls are formed by the infilling of tissues between branches.

questions.explanation: Microphylls in club mosses (lycopsida) evolved from the infilling of simple tissues between lateral branches. This process led to the formation of small, leaf-like structures that are characteristic of microphylls. So option D is correct.

questions.text: In the given diagram:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This structure does not contain antheridia and archegonia.
This structure does not contain antheridia and archegonia.
Option C shows gametophyte with embedded antheridia and archegonia.
Option D represents rhizoids that don't contain antheridia and archegonia.

questions.explanation: Option C shows gametophyte with embedded antheridia and archegonia.So C is the correct option.

questions.text: In the chloroplasts of plant cells, a large number of antenna pigment molecules in the photosystems, embedded in the thylakoid membrane, allow the:
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: Reaction centre to gain the greatest possible amount of energy
options.explanation: Antennae pigment molecules in the photosystem help channel light energy to the reaction center, maximizing energy absorption.

options.text: Light energy to be harvested in the molecule of electron acceptor
options.explanation: While light energy is eventually transferred to electron acceptors, the primary role of antennae pigments is to concentrate energy in the reaction center first.

options.text: Accessory pigment molecules to get closely associated with each other
options.explanation: The closeness of accessory pigments is not the main function of antennae pigments. Their primary role is to capture and funnel light to the reaction center.

options.text: Chlorophyll-b molecule in the reaction centre to become highly energised
options.explanation: Chlorophyll-b is not located in the reaction center. The primary role of antennae pigments is to energize the reaction center where chlorophyll-a is located.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option A. Antennae pigment molecules in the photosystem are responsible for capturing light energy and funneling it to the reaction center. This process maximizes the amount of energy that the reaction center can gain, which is crucial for efficient photosynthesis.

questions.text: In the light dependent reactions of photosynthesis, both cyclic and non-cyclic flow of electrons take place. The cyclic flow of electrons is called ‘cyclic’ because, after the production of:
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: ATP molecules, the electrons are cycled back into the photosystem I
options.explanation: This option is correct. In cyclic electron flow, ATP is produced, and the electrons are cycled back to photosystem I.

options.text: ATP molecules, the electrons are cycled back into the photosystem II
options.explanation: This option is incorrect. Cyclic electron flow involves photosystem I, not photosystem II, and results in the production of ATP but not NADPH.

options.text: NADPH molecules, the electrons are cycled back into the photosystem I
options.explanation: This option is incorrect. In cyclic electron flow, NADPH is not produced; only ATP is generated.

options.text: NADPH molecules, the electrons are cycled back into the photosystem II
options.explanation: This option is incorrect. Cyclic electron flow does not produce NADPH, and it involves photosystem I rather than photosystem II.

questions.explanation: The correct understanding involves recognizing that in the cyclic flow of electrons during the light-dependent reactions of photosynthesis, electrons are cycled back to photosystem I, not photosystem II, after ATP production. This process does not produce NADPH, as it is focused solely on ATP generation.

questions.text: Consider the given reaction which occurs in the leaves of C4 plants.
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The C4 cycle reactions do not occur within the xylem vessels of plants.

The C4 cycle, also known as the Hatch-Slack pathway, has two main steps: initial carbon fixation in mesophyll cells and the subsequent Calvin cycle in bundle sheath cells. Carbon fixation does not occur in bundle sheath cells.

The C4 cycle, also known as the Hatch-Slack pathway, has two main steps: initial carbon fixation in mesophyll cells and the subsequent Calvin cycle in bundle sheath cells. Carbon fixation doesn't occur in sieve tubes.

The C4 cycle, also known as the Hatch-Slack pathway, has two main steps: initial carbon fixation in mesophyll cells and the subsequent Calvin cycle in bundle sheath cells. So mesophyll cells is correct option.

questions.explanation: The C4 cycle, also known as the Hatch-Slack pathway, has two main steps: initial carbon fixation in mesophyll cells and the subsequent Calvin cycle in bundle sheath cells. As this reaction shows initial carbon fixation, it must occur in mesophyll cells. So D is correct.

questions.text: The given diagram summarises the reactions of anaerobic respiration:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This process occurs in alcoholic fermentation, but ATP is not produced during this conversion.
This stage is known as glycolysis, where ATP is generated as glucose is broken down into pyruvate.
ATP is not produced during the conversion of pyruvic acid to lactic acid in lactic acid fermentation.
Carbon dioxide release occurs in alcoholic fermentation, but it is not associated with ATP production.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is the formation of pyruvic acid from glucose. This step is known as glycolysis, which is the initial stage of both aerobic and anaerobic respiration. During glycolysis, glucose is broken down into two molecules of pyruvate, and ATP is produced. In anaerobic conditions, pyruvate can be further converted into either ethanol or lactic acid, but these subsequent steps do not produce ATP. Therefore, the only stage in anaerobic respiration where ATP is generated is during the formation of pyruvic acid from glucose.

questions.text: In the digestive system of cockroach, the function of gizzard is to:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The gizzard is a muscular organ that helps in the mechanical breakdown of food by grinding it, often with the help of ingested particles or grit.
This function is primarily associated with the crop in the digestive system, not the gizzard.
The secretion of digestive enzymes is mainly the role of the salivary glands and the midgut, not the gizzard.
Reabsorbing water is related to the function of the hindgut, particularly the rectum, not the gizzard.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option A.The gizzard in a cockroach is a thick-walled muscular organ that mechanically breaks down food by grinding it. This process is aided by the presence of chitinous teeth or plates. This function is crucial for the digestive process as it prepares the food for further enzymatic digestion.

questions.text: All of the following are the constituents of human saliva EXCEPT
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Mucus is present in saliva and helps in lubricating the oral cavity.
Sucrose is not a natural component of saliva; it is a type of sugar that can be ingested through food.
Amylase is an enzyme in saliva that begins the digestion of carbohydrates.
Sodium bicarbonate is present in saliva and helps neutralize acids in the mouth.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is sucrose. Human saliva contains mucus to lubricate the mouth, amylase to begin the digestion of starches, and sodium bicarbonate to help neutralize acids. Sucrose, however, is not naturally found in saliva; it is a sugar that is introduced to the mouth through food and drink. The other options, mucus, amylase, and sodium bicarbonate, are all natural components of saliva with specific functions.

questions.text: Venus flytrap is an insectivorous plant in which the organ specialised to trap insects is
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The leaves of the Venus flytrap are modified to form traps that capture and digest insects.
Buds are primarily involved in the development of new leaves or flowers, not in trapping insects.
Roots are typically used for water and nutrient absorption, not for trapping insects.
Branches are structural parts of plants, usually supporting leaves and flowers, not specialized for trapping insects.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Leaves. The Venus flytrap is known for its specialized leaves that act as traps. These leaves snap shut when trigger hairs are stimulated by the presence of an insect, allowing the plant to trap and digest its prey for nutrients. The other options are incorrect as follows: Buds are involved in the growth of new plant parts; Roots are involved in nutrient uptake and are not used for trapping; Branches support other structures but do not trap insects.

questions.text: The structure that does NOT contribute to the large surface area of the ileum is the presence of
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Villi are finger-like projections that significantly increase the surface area of the ileum for nutrient absorption.
Microvilli are tiny hair-like structures on the surface of epithelial cells that further increase the surface area. Although they contribute to surface area, they are not the correct option here.
The muscularis layer contains muscles that help in the movement of the intestine but do not contribute to increasing the surface area.

options.text: Circular folds in the mucosal layer
options.explanation: Circular folds, also known as plicae circulares, are folds in the mucosal layer that increase the surface area available for absorption.

questions.explanation: The correct answer ismuscles in the muscularis layerThe muscularis layer's primary function is to aid in the movement of the intestines through peristalsis. It does not have a role in increasing the surface area for absorption. On the other hand, villi, microvilli, and circular folds all serve to increase the surface area of the ileum, enhancing the absorption of nutrients.

questions.text: The given diagram shows the difference between the countercurrent and concurrent exchangesystem in fish:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: In Counter-current exchange, water and blood flow in opposite directions over the gill lamellae(bidirectional). In Concurrent exchange, water and blood flowing in the same direction(unidirectional).
The pressure gradient is not important here rather O2 concentration gradient is important
Contact time is same in both systems.

Counter current exchange ensures blood is always exposed to water with a higher oxygen concentration. Concurrent exchange quickly equalizes the oxygen concentration, reducing the efficiency of oxygen uptake.

questions.explanation: In fish, countercurrent exchange is a highly efficient system for gas exchange in the gills, where water and blood flow in opposite directions, maximizing oxygen uptake. Concurrent exchange, on the other hand, involves water and blood flowing in the same direction, resulting in a less efficient transfer of oxygen. Counter current exchange ensures blood is always exposed to water with a higher oxygen concentration. Concurrent exchange quickly equalizes the oxygen concentration, reducing the efficiency of oxygen uptake.So option D is correct.

questions.text: In the chest X-ray of a patient, cavities in the lungs was observed due to a chronic bacterialinfection.On the basis of this observation, the disease is diagnosed as
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Tuberculosis is characterized by the formation of cavities in the lungs due to chronic bacterial infection, often visible on chest X-rays.
Emphysema involves damage to the air sacs in the lungs, not typically associated with cavities or a bacterial infection.
Lung cancer can cause various changes in the lung structure but is not specifically characterized by cavities due to bacterial infection.
Pneumonia is an infection that inflames the air sacs in one or both lungs, which may fill with fluid, but it doesn't typically cause cavities.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Tuberculosis. Tuberculosis (TB) is a chronic bacterial infection caused by Mycobacterium tuberculosis, which can lead to the formation of cavities in the lungs. These cavities are visible in chest X-rays and are a hallmark of pulmonary TB.Emphysema is a form of chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD) characterized by damaged alveoli, not by bacterial infection or cavities. Lung cancer can cause a variety of lung changes, but it does not typically result in cavities caused by infection. Pneumonia is an infection that causes fluid accumulation in the lungs, but it usually doesn't cause cavitary lesions.

questions.text: What happens to the muscles of diaphragm and internal intercostal muscles during exhalationin humans?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This is incorrect. While the internal intercostal muscles contract, the diaphragm actually relaxes during exhalation.
This is incorrect. During exhalation, the diaphragm relaxes instead of contracting and internal intercostal muscles contract.
This is incorrect. The internal intercostal muscles contract during exhalation, while only the diaphragm relaxes.
This is correct. During exhalation, the diaphragm relaxes and the internal intercostal muscles contract to decrease thoracic volume.

questions.explanation: During exhalation in humans, the diaphragm relaxes, causing it to move upwards into the thoracic cavity. This reduces the volume of the thoracic cavity, pushing air out of the lungs. Simultaneously, the internal intercostal muscles contract, which further reduces the volume of the thoracic cavity by pulling the ribs down and inwards. Option D is correct because it accurately describes these muscle actions. Options A, C, and B are incorrect because they do not accurately describe the relaxation and contraction pattern of these muscles during exhalation.

questions.text: During atrial systole in the cardiac cycle of humans, the state of ventricles, atrioventricularvalves and semilunar valves is
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: During atrial systole, the ventricles are relaxed, the atrioventricular valves are open to allow blood into the ventricles, and the semilunar valves remain closed not open. Hence this option is incorrect.
This is correct because during atrial systole, the ventricles are relaxed, the atrioventricular valves are open to allow blood into the ventricles, and the semilunar valves remain closed.
During atrial systole, the ventricles are relaxed not contracted, the atrioventricular valves are open not close to allow blood into the ventricles, and the semilunar valves remain closed.
During atrial systole, the ventricles are relaxed not contracted, the atrioventricular valves are open not closed to allow blood into the ventricles, and the semilunar valves remain closed not open.

questions.explanation: During atrial systole, the atria contract to push blood into the ventricles. Therefore, the ventricles are relaxed at this stage. The atrioventricular valves (tricuspid and mitral valves) must be open to allow blood to flow from the atria into the ventricles. Meanwhile, the semilunar valves (aortic and pulmonary valves) remain closed to prevent blood from flowing back into the ventricles from the arteries. Hence, Option B is correct. Options A, C, and D describe states that do not occur during atrial systole.
hints: the state of ventricles, atrioventricular valves and semilunar valves is

questions.text: The given diagram shows the water potentials of a plant cell and the solution in which it wasimmersed for 10 minutes:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: In general, water moves from an area of higher water potential to an area of lower water potential. So water will move from the solution into cell, water potential of cell will become higher and cell will become turgid.
In general, water moves from an area of higher water potential to an area of lower water potential. So water will move into the cell but resultant effects mentioned are incorrect.
In general, water moves from an area of higher water potential to an area of lower water potential. So water will not move out of the cell.
In general, water moves from an area of higher water potential to an area of lower water potential. So water will not move out of the cell.

questions.explanation: In general, water moves from an area of higher water potential to an area of lower water potential. So water will move from the solution into cell, water potential of cell will become higher and cell will become turgid.So, option A is correct.

questions.text: The given graph shows the level of antibody in blood serum after vaccination and an exposure to the same antigen a few days later. The antibody concentration is higher in secondary immune response because at this point, the
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Innate immunity refers to the body's first line of defense against pathogens, involving non-specific responses. It does not relate to antibody levels after vaccination.
Passive immunity involves the transfer of antibodies from one individual to another, such as from mother to child. It does not explain the increased antibody response after vaccination.
The primary immune response occurs when the immune system first encounters an antigen, leading to a slower and lower antibody production compared to the secondary response.
The secondary immune response occurs when the immune system encounters the same antigen again, leading to a faster and more robust production of antibodies due to the presence of memory cells.

questions.explanation: Secondary response is faster and stronger due to memory cells.

questions.text: If a plant cell with a solute potential of −0.65 kPa is placed in a solution having water potentialof −3.65 kPa, then its pressure potential will be:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: mathematically incorrect.
Water potential = solute potential + pressure potentialThis formula gives a pressure potential of 3kPa not 4.3kPa.
Water potential = solute potential + pressure potentialThis formula gives a pressure potential of -3.0kPa. which is correct.
Water potential = solute potential + pressure potentialThis formula gives a pressure potential of 3kPa not -4.3kPa.

questions.explanation: Water potential = solute potential + pressure potential-3.65+(-0.65)= pressure potentialPressure potential = -3.0kpaSo C is correct option.

questions.text: An escape of blood from a ruptured blood vessel within the human body is termed as
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: A haemorrhage refers to the escape of blood from a ruptured blood vessel, making it the correct answer.
Atherosclerosis is the buildup of fats, cholesterol, and other substances in and on the artery walls, leading to restricted blood flow. It does not refer to the escape of blood.
Arteriosclerosis is the thickening, hardening, and loss of elasticity of the walls of the arteries. It does not involve any rupture or escape of blood.

Myocardial infarction, also known as a heart attack, occurs when blood flow to a part of the heart is blocked for a long enough time that part of the heart muscle is damaged or dies. It does not specifically refer to blood escaping from a vessel.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is haemorrhage. A haemorrhage is the medical term for the escape of blood from a ruptured blood vessel. This can occur in any part of the body. The other options describe different medical conditions: atherosclerosis involves the buildup of substances in arteries; arteriosclerosis refers to the hardening of the arteries; and myocardial infarction is a heart attack caused by blocked blood flow, not by a ruptured vessel.

questions.text: Presence of aerenchyma in the leaves, stem and roots of plants is an anatomical adaptation inI. hydrophytesII. mesophytesIII. halophytes
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Aerenchyma is typically found in hydrophytes, which are plants adapted to living in aquatic environments.
Mesophytes are plants that live in moderate environments and generally do not have aerenchyma.
While aerenchyma is found in hydrophytes, it is not a common adaptation in halophytes.
Neither mesophytes nor halophytes typically have aerenchyma as an adaptation.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'I only.' Aerenchyma is a type of tissue with large air spaces that facilitates gas exchange, particularly in aquatic environments. This adaptation is common in hydrophytes, which are plants that grow in water. Mesophytes, which grow in moderate environments, and halophytes, which are adapted to saline conditions, do not typically have aerenchyma as a significant anatomical adaptation. Therefore, the presence of aerenchyma is specifically an adaptation for hydrophytes.

questions.text: The urine test report of a person shows the presence of free haemoglobin in urine, indicating that the red blood cells have been lysed.The part of nephron that MOST LIKELY shows malfunctioning in the given situation is
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The glomerulus is responsible for the initial filtration of blood, but it is not involved in the reabsorption or handling of hemoglobin from lysed red blood cells.
The loop of Henle primarily functions in concentrating urine and reabsorbing water and salts, not in processing lysed red blood cells or free hemoglobin.
The collecting duct is involved in the final concentration of urine, not in handling hemoglobin from lysed red blood cells.

The proximal convoluted tubule is involved in reabsorbing nutrients, water, and electrolytes. It is responsible for reabsorbing filtered substances, including hemoglobin from lysed red blood cells, making it likely to show malfunction in this scenario.

questions.explanation: The presence of free hemoglobin in urine suggests that hemoglobin is not being properly reabsorbed or processed by the nephron. The proximal convoluted tubule is responsible for reabsorbing the majority of substances from the filtrate, including proteins like hemoglobin. If this part of the nephron is malfunctioning, it can lead to the presence of free hemoglobin in urine. The glomerulus is responsible for filtering blood, but it does not reabsorb hemoglobin. The loop of Henle and the collecting duct are primarily involved in concentrating urine, not in processing hemoglobin.

questions.text: In the human kidney, the part of nephron where receptors for antidiuretic hormone (ADH) are found is
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The glomerulus is involved in the filtration of blood, not the site for ADH receptors.
The ascending loop of Henle is primarily involved in the reabsorption of ions like sodium and chloride, not the primary site for ADH action.
The descending loop of Henle is mainly permeable to water but does not have receptors for ADH.
The collecting duct is the correct answer because it contains receptors for ADH, which helps regulate water reabsorption.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is the collecting duct. The collecting duct is where the receptors for antidiuretic hormone (ADH) are located. ADH binds to these receptors to increase water reabsorption, thus concentrating the urine. The glomerulus, ascending loop of Henle, and descending loop of Henle do not have ADH receptors; they play different roles in the filtration and reabsorption processes within the nephron.

questions.text: Following are the characteristics of a plant tissue.I. It has angular thickening in its primary cell wall.II. It provides support to young herbaceous parts of the plant.The plant tissue with these characteristics is identified as
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Sclereids are a type of sclerenchyma cell known for their thick secondary walls and role in providing hardness and rigidity, not specifically angular thickening or support for young parts.
Tracheids are elongated cells in the xylem that help in the transportation of water and minerals, not primarily known for angular thickening or supporting young herbaceous parts.
Parenchyma cells generally have thin cell walls and are involved in photosynthesis, storage, and tissue repair, not angular thickening or support.
Collenchyma cells are characterized by their unevenly thickened primary cell walls and provide structural support to growing parts of the plant, such as young stems and leaves.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is collenchyma. Collenchyma tissues are specialized for providing support to young, growing parts of the plant, such as stems and leaves, which aligns with the characteristic of providing support to young herbaceous parts. They are characterized by their uneven or angular thickening of the primary cell walls, which helps them support and strengthen these parts without hindering growth.On the other hand, sclereids are a form of sclerenchyma with heavily lignified walls, contributing to hardness and rigidity, not the flexible support described. Tracheids are involved in water transport and structural support but do not match the description of angular thickening or support specifically for young herbaceous parts. Parenchyma cells are generally thin-walled and involved in various functions like storage and photosynthesis, not specifically support with angular thickening.

questions.text: The set of structures which comprises human axial skeleton is
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This option correctly lists the components of the human axial skeleton.
This option includes bones that are part of the appendicular skeleton, such as the humerus and femur.

options.text: Skull, pectoral girdle, pelvic girdle and ribs
options.explanation: This option incorrectly includes the pectoral and pelvic girdles, which are part of the appendicular skeleton.

options.text: Vertebrae, clavicle, shoulder blade and humerus
options.explanation: This option lists bones that are primarily part of the appendicular skeleton, except for the vertebrae.

questions.explanation: The axial skeleton consists of the skull, vertebrae, ribs, and sternum. These structures form the central framework of the body, supporting and protecting vital organs. Option A correctly lists these components. Option B includes humerus, femur, and phalanges, which are part of the appendicular skeleton. Option C incorrectly lists the pectoral and pelvic girdles, which also belong to the appendicular skeleton. Option D includes the clavicle, shoulder blade, and humerus, which are not part of the axial skeleton.

questions.text: The hormone(s) synthesised in the meristematic tissue of the plant shoot and root is/ areI. Abscisic acidII. AuxinIII. Cytokinin
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Abscisic acid is primarily associated with stress responses and not synthesized in meristematic tissues.
Auxin is indeed synthesized in the meristematic tissues of plant shoots and roots.
While auxin is synthesized in meristematic tissues, abscisic acid is not typically synthesized there.
Both auxin and cytokinin are synthesized in meristematic tissues of plant shoots and roots, playing key roles in plant growth and development.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is D. II and III. Both auxin and cytokinin are hormones synthesized in meristematic tissues of plants. Auxin is primarily responsible for cell elongation and is synthesized at the tips of shoots and roots. Cytokinin promotes cell division and differentiation and is also synthesized in these meristematic areas. Abscisic acid, on the other hand, is mainly involved in stress responses and is not typically synthesized in meristematic tissues, making options A, B, and C incorrect.

questions.text: Deciduous plants lose their leaves each year in response to changes in the day length.The biorhythm exemplified in the given statement is
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Circadian rhythms are biological cycles that occur approximately every 24 hours, such as sleep-wake cycles.
Circannual rhythms are biological cycles that occur annually, like the shedding of leaves by deciduous plants in response to seasonal changes.
Circatidal rhythms are biological cycles that follow the tides, typically happening twice a day.
Circalunar rhythms are biological cycles that follow the lunar month, such as certain reproductive cycles in marine organisms.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option B: circannual rhythm. This is because deciduous plants lose their leaves once a year, which aligns with the annual cycle characteristic of circannual rhythms.Option A, circadian rhythm, is incorrect because it involves daily cycles. Option C, circatidal rhythm, is incorrect because it involves tidal patterns, typically occurring multiple times a day. Option D, circalunar rhythm, is incorrect because it involves monthly cycles related to the lunar phases, not annual changes.

questions.text: An example of the monosynaptic reflex in humans is
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The pupil reflex involves multiple synapses and is controlled by the autonomic nervous system, making it a polysynaptic reflex.
The knee-jerk (or patellar) reflex is a classic example of a monosynaptic reflex, involving a direct communication between the sensory and motor neurons.
Foot withdrawal in response to pain involves multiple synapses and interneurons, classifying it as a polysynaptic reflex.

options.text: Hand withdrawal when touched by a hot object
options.explanation: Hand withdrawal when touching a hot object is a polysynaptic reflex involving multiple synapses and pathways.

questions.explanation: The knee-jerk stretch reflex is the correct answer because it is a monosynaptic reflex, where the sensory neuron directly communicates with the motor neuron in the spinal cord without the involvement of interneurons. The pupil reflex, foot withdrawal, and hand withdrawal are all polysynaptic, involving multiple synapses and more complex neural pathways.

questions.text: In the human brain, the part that process long-term memory is
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The thalamus acts primarily as a relay station for sensory and motor signals to the cerebral cortex. It is not primarily involved in long-term memory processing.
The amygdala is mainly associated with emotions and emotional memory, not the general processing of long-term memory.
The hippocampus is crucial for the formation, organization, and storage of long-term memories. It is the correct answer.
The hypothalamus regulates various autonomic processes, including hunger, thirst, and body temperature, but does not primarily process long-term memory.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is the hippocampus, which plays a vital role in the formation and retrieval of long-term memories. It is part of the limbic system and is essential for memory consolidation. The other options are incorrect because they serve different primary functions: the thalamus is a relay station for sensory and motor signals, the amygdala is involved in emotion and emotional memory, and the hypothalamus regulates autonomic functions such as temperature and hunger.

questions.text: Autoimmune destruction of adrenal cortex is the most common cause of
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Cushing's disease is characterized by excessive cortisol production, usually due to a pituitary adenoma, not autoimmune destruction.
Addison's disease, also known as primary adrenal insufficiency, is most commonly caused by autoimmune destruction of the adrenal cortex.
Grave's disease is an autoimmune disorder affecting the thyroid gland, resulting in hyperthyroidism, not related to the adrenal cortex.
Acromegaly results from excessive growth hormone, typically due to a pituitary tumor, and is unrelated to adrenal cortex destruction.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Addison’s disease. It is a condition where the adrenal glands are unable to produce sufficient steroid hormones due to autoimmune destruction of the adrenal cortex. This autoimmune process is the most common cause of Addison's disease. In contrast, Cushing’s disease involves excessive cortisol due to a pituitary issue, Grave’s disease is related to thyroid gland hyperactivity, and Acromegaly is associated with excessive growth hormone production, neither of which involve the adrenal cortex.

questions.text: Given are the different types of hormones produced in the human body.I. TestosteroneII. CorticosteroidsIII. Luteinizing hormone (LH)IV. Gonadotropin-releasing hormone (GnRH)The hyposecretion of hormones that cause infertility in males are
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Testosterone and corticosteroids do not both lead to infertility when hyposecreted. Testosterone is crucial for male fertility, but corticosteroids are not directly related.
Corticosteroids are not directly involved in male fertility, while luteinizing hormone (LH) plays a crucial role in stimulating testosterone production.
This combination includes testosterone, which is crucial for fertility, but corticosteroids are not directly related to infertility issues.
Testosterone, luteinizing hormone (LH), and gonadotropin-releasing hormone (GnRH) are all involved in male reproductive health. Hyposecretion of these can potentially lead to infertility.

questions.explanation: The hyposecretion of testosterone, luteinizing hormone (LH), and gonadotropin-releasing hormone (GnRH) can lead to infertility in males. Testosterone is essential for sperm production and secondary sexual characteristics. LH stimulates the production of testosterone in the testes. GnRH controls the release of LH and follicle-stimulating hormone (FSH), both of which are crucial for reproductive function. Corticosteroids, while important for stress response and other functions, are not directly related to fertility.

questions.text: When new animals are brought to zoos, initially they get scared, but after sometime they become familiar to the environment.The type of learning behaviour exemplified in the given statement is
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Imprinting is a form of learning typically occurring at a specific life stage, where an animal forms attachments and develops a concept of its identity. It is not related to gradual adaptation to an environment.

Habituation is the process by which an animal becomes accustomed to a stimulus through repeated exposure, leading to a decrease in response. This is the correct form of learning behavior where animals become familiar with their environment over time.

Latent learning refers to knowledge that is acquired without any obvious reinforcement or immediate expression, which may be demonstrated later when needed, not necessarily related to becoming familiar with an environment.

Insight learning involves problem-solving through understanding the relationships between various parts of a problem, often in a sudden realization, rather than through gradual adaptation to an environment.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is habituation. Habituation is a simple form of learning where an animal, through repeated exposure, becomes accustomed to a stimulus and its response diminishes over time. In the context of the question, new animals in zoos initially get scared but eventually become familiar with their surroundings, demonstrating habituation. Imprinting is incorrect because it involves forming strong attachments during a critical period, not adapting to new environments. Latent learning is knowledge that is not immediately expressed, whereas insight learning involves sudden problem-solving abilities, neither of which applies to the gradual familiarization process described.

questions.text: Red-green colour blindness is a recessive X-linked trait in humans.The set of parents that are MOST LIKELY to produce a daughter with red-green colour blindness is a/ an:
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: Carrier mother and a colour blind father
options.explanation: A carrier mother has one normal X chromosome and one affected X chromosome. A color blind father has only one X chromosome, which is affected. This pair can produce a daughter who is color blind if she inherits the affected X from both parents.

options.text: Carrier mother and an unaffected father
options.explanation: An unaffected father only has a normal X chromosome to pass on. A carrier mother can pass on either a normal or affected X. There is a 50% chance the daughter will be a carrier, but she cannot be color blind.

options.text: Colour blind mother and an unaffected father
options.explanation: A color blind mother has two affected X chromosomes. An unaffected father has a normal X chromosome to pass on. This pair will produce daughters who are carriers, not color blind.

options.text: Unaffected, non-carrier mother and a colour blind father
options.explanation: An unaffected, non-carrier mother only has normal X chromosomes. A color blind father can only pass on the affected X chromosome to his daughters, making them carriers, but not color blind.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is a carrier mother and a color blind father. A carrier mother has one normal X chromosome and one affected X chromosome, while a color blind father has the affected X chromosome. If the daughter inherits the affected X from both parents, she will be color blind. Other combinations either result in daughters who are carriers or unaffected, as they would inherit at least one normal X chromosome, which masks the effect of the recessive X-linked trait.

questions.text: Given are the Rh blood groups of a couple.I.Father → Rh positive.II.Mother → Rh negative.The mother conceives an Rh positive child. In the given scenario, the chances of mother-foetus Rh incompatibility will be:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This is incorrect. There is a chance of Rh incompatibility since the mother is Rh negative and the child is Rh positive.
This option is incorrect. The probability of incompatibility is not a simple percentage like 25%. It depends on the specific interaction of the Rh factors.
This is incorrect. While genetic probabilities often use 50%, this does not apply to the biological mechanism of Rh incompatibility.
This is the correct option. The mother-foetus Rh incompatibility will occur because the mother's immune system may start producing antibodies against the Rh-positive blood of the foetus.

questions.explanation: In the given scenario, the mother is Rh-negative, and the child is Rh-positive. This situation can lead to Rh incompatibility, where the mother's immune system may produce antibodies against the Rh-positive blood cells of the foetus, potentially causing haemolytic disease of the newborn. Therefore, the chances of mother-foetus Rh incompatibility are 100%. Option A (0%) is incorrect because there is a definite risk of incompatibility. Option B (25%) and Option C (50%) are also incorrect because these percentages do not accurately represent the biological risk involved.

questions.text: A chromosome have two genes X and Y. Gene Y influences the phenotypic effect of gene X.Based on the influential phenotypic effect, the gene Y is:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Genes that are linked are located close to each other on the same chromosome and tend to be inherited together, but this doesn't specifically imply a phenotypic influence.
Epistasis occurs when the effect of one gene (gene Y) masks or modifies the phenotypic effect of another gene (gene X), which is the correct relationship described.
A hypostatic gene is one whose phenotypic effect is masked or modified by an epistatic gene; here, gene X would be hypostatic to gene Y, not the other way around.

options.text: Independent of gene X for its expression
options.explanation: If gene Y were independent of gene X, it would not influence the expression of gene X, contrary to what is described.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is epistatic to gene X. Epistasis is a genetic interaction where the phenotype of one gene (gene Y) affects or masks the expression of another gene (gene X). This is different from linked genes, which are simply located near each other on the chromosome and do not necessarily influence each other's phenotypic expression. Hypostatic refers to the gene that is being masked (in this case, gene X), and independence would mean gene Y does not affect gene X at all, which is not the scenario described.

questions.text: The recombination frequency between two genes of a chromosome is directly proportional to the:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The length of the chromosome is not directly proportional to the recombination frequency between two specific genes.
Physical linkage refers to genes located on the same chromosome, but recombination frequency is more specifically related to the distance between them.
Protein content of a chromosome is unrelated to the recombination frequency between genes.

options.text: Distance between the linked gene loci
options.explanation: The recombination frequency increases with the distance between the linked gene loci, as there is a higher chance of crossover events occurring between them.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is the 'distance between the linked gene loci.' Recombination frequency is a measure of how often crossing over occurs between two genes during meiosis. The farther apart two genes are on a chromosome, the more likely it is that a crossover will occur between them, leading to a higher recombination frequency. The other options are incorrect because they do not directly impact the likelihood of recombination events.

questions.text: The pattern of sex determination in which the female is heterogametic is found in:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Protenor bugs have a different mechanism, where the sex determination is based on the presence or absence of a sex chromosome (XO system), not female heterogamety.
In grasshoppers, males are heterogametic (XO), not females.
In butterflies, the females are heterogametic (ZW), which matches the condition described in the question.
In fruit flies (Drosophila), the sex determination system is similar to humans, where males are the heterogametic sex (XY).

questions.explanation: The correct answer is butterflies. In the ZW sex-determination system, which is observed in butterflies, moths, and birds, the female is heterogametic (ZW) and the male is homogametic (ZZ). This contrasts with the XY system in humans and fruit flies where the male is heterogametic. Protenor bugs and grasshoppers use the XO system, which involves different mechanisms of sex determination.

questions.text: It is observed that pea plants with white flowers have colourless seed coats and leaf axils. This shows that the gene for flower colour affects more than one characteristics.The given observation exemplifies the:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Epistatic inheritance occurs when one gene's expression is masked by another gene. This is not the case here, as the observation involves a single gene affecting multiple traits, not gene masking.
Polygenic inheritance involves multiple genes affecting a single trait, which is the opposite of the observation where one gene affects multiple traits.

Pleiotropic inheritance describes a situation where one gene influences multiple phenotypic traits. This is consistent with the observation of a single gene affecting flower color, seed coat color, and leaf axil color.
Incomplete dominance occurs when the heterozygous phenotype is intermediate between the two homozygous phenotypes. This is not relevant to the observation of one gene affecting multiple traits.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is pleiotropic inheritance. This genetic phenomenon occurs when a single gene influences multiple phenotypic traits, as seen in the observation where the gene for flower color also affects seed coat and leaf axil colors. This differs from epistatic inheritance, where one gene masks the expression of another, and polygenic inheritance, where multiple genes affect a single trait. Incomplete dominance is unrelated, as it involves blending of traits in heterozygotes.

questions.text: In plants, reproduction through the process of cuttings is advantageous as compared to reproduction through seeds because it
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Reproduction through cuttings does not increase the chances of mutation. In fact, it typically reduces genetic variability, which decreases mutation rates.
Cuttings allow plants to rapidly produce offspring that are identical to the parent, which are already well-adapted to the current environment.
Reproduction through cuttings produces genetically identical offspring, which does not contribute to genetic variability like sexual reproduction does.

options.text: Produces identical offspring in short time
options.explanation: While this option is true, it does not directly address the advantage of environmental adaptation. It highlights the efficiency of cuttings but not their adaptive benefits.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option B: facilitates adaptation to environment. Reproduction through cuttings produces offspring that are genetically identical to the parent, which helps them retain the parent's adaptations to the current environment, ensuring survival and stability.Option A is incorrect because cuttings do not increase mutation rates; they maintain genetic consistency. Option C is incorrect because cuttings produce uniform offspring, not genetic variability. Option D, while true about the speed and uniformity of reproduction, doesn't emphasize the environmental adaptation aspect.

questions.text: The event which does NOT occur in the life cycle of gymnosperms is the formation of:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Gymnosperms form pollen tubes to transport sperm cells to the egg cells.
Gymnosperms do not produce two male gametes; this is characteristic of angiosperms during double fertilization.
Gymnosperms form an endosperm before fertilisation.
Gymnosperms do produce multi-nucleated female gametophytes during their reproductive cycle.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option B: two male gametes.In gymnosperms, unlike angiosperms, there is no double fertilization event that results in the formation of two male gametes. Instead, gymnosperms typically produce one sperm cell that fertilizes the egg.

questions.text: The time period between fertilisation and birth in mammals is termed as
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Gestation is the period between fertilization and birth in mammals, during which the embryo develops in the uterus.
Ovulation is the release of an egg from the ovary, which occurs before fertilization.
Conception refers to the process of fertilization when the sperm meets the egg, marking the beginning of pregnancy.
Implantation is the stage where the fertilized egg attaches to the wall of the uterus, which occurs shortly after conception.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is gestation. Gestation is the period from fertilization until birth in mammals, where the embryo develops into a fetus. Ovulation is the process of releasing an egg, which precedes fertilization. Conception is the actual fertilization of the egg by the sperm, which marks the start of pregnancy. Implantation occurs when the fertilized egg attaches to the uterus, which is an early stage of pregnancy but not the entire period.

questions.text: Different types of supportive cells in human testis are involved inI. The production of testosterone.II. Providing structural and metabolic support to spermatogenic cells.III. Providing nourishment to the developing sperms.The function(s) of sertoli cells is/ are
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Testosterone production is primarily the function of Leydig cells, not Sertoli cells.
While Sertoli cells do provide structural and metabolic support, they also nourish developing sperms, making this option incomplete.
This option mistakenly attributes testosterone production to Sertoli cells, which is incorrect.
Sertoli cells provide both structural and metabolic support, as well as nourishment to developing sperms, making this the correct choice.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is II and III. Sertoli cells are responsible for providing structural and metabolic support to spermatogenic cells and also nourish developing sperm cells. Option I is incorrect because the production of testosterone is primarily the function of Leydig cells, not Sertoli cells. Option B is incorrect because it only includes one of the functions of Sertoli cells. Option C is incorrect because it includes the erroneous function of testosterone production.

questions.text: A nucleosome is composed of
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: A nucleosome is indeed composed of DNA wrapped around histone proteins.
RNA is not a component of nucleosomes; nucleosomes are primarily composed of DNA and histones.
While these are components of nucleotides, they do not form nucleosomes.
These are the components of a nucleotide, not a nucleosome.

questions.explanation: A nucleosome is the fundamental unit of DNA packaging in eukaryotic cells, consisting of a segment of DNA wound around a core of histone proteins. Option A is correct because it accurately describes this structure. Option B is incorrect because RNA is not involved in forming nucleosomes. Options C and D describe components of nucleotides, not the structure of nucleosomes.

questions.text: In contrast to heterochromatin, euchromatin is
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Heterochromatin, not euchromatin, is typically stained darker due to its condensed structure.
Euchromatin is less condensed, allowing for active transcription of genes.
Euchromatin is actually the more active form of chromatin, involved in gene transcription.
While euchromatin can become more condensed during cell division, it is generally less condensed than heterochromatin.

questions.explanation: Euchromatin is less condensed compared to heterochromatin and is actively involved in transcription. It represents the portion of the genome where transcription is taking place, making it 'active' chromatin. In contrast, heterochromatin is more condensed and typically not actively transcribed, forming a 'silent' or inactive chromatin. So option D is correct.

questions.text: In sexually reproducing organisms, before fertilisation, gametes are formed by reduction division toI. increase the genetic variation.II. reduce the chances of mutation.III. ensure the diploid number of chromosomes in zygote.
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: While reduction division can contribute to genetic variation, this option ignores the main purpose of maintaining chromosome numbers.
Reduction division primarily ensures that gametes have half the chromosome number, so the zygote has the correct diploid number.
This option is incorrect as it includes point II, which is not the primary function of reduction division.
Point II is incorrect as reduction division is not primarily about reducing mutation chances; it's about maintaining chromosome numbers.

questions.explanation: Reduction division, or meiosis, occurs in sexually reproducing organisms to produce gametes with half the chromosome number. This ensures that when fertilization occurs, the resulting zygote has the correct diploid chromosome number (III). While meiosis introduces genetic variation, its primary function is not to increase genetic variation, which makes option B (III only) the correct answer. The chances of mutation are not reduced in meiosis.

questions.text: An example that demonstrates apoptosis is
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: A severe burn causes skin cells to die
options.explanation: This is not apoptosis. Skin cell death from a burn is due to necrosis, which is an uncontrolled process caused by external damage.

options.text: The self-destruction of an immune cell
options.explanation: This is an example of apoptosis. Immune cells undergo programmed cell death to maintain homeostasis and prevent immune overreaction.

options.text: The irreversible loss of the liver cells in liver cancer
options.explanation: This is not apoptosis. In liver cancer, the loss of liver cells is associated with uncontrolled cell growth and malignancy.

options.text: An over stretched muscle fibre due to heavy exercise
options.explanation: This is not apoptosis. Stretching of muscle fibers from exercise results in physical damage or stress, not programmed cell death.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option B:the self-destruction of an immune cell.Apoptosis is a programmed process of cell death that occurs in multicellular organisms, helping to remove cells that are no longer needed or are a threat to the organism, like immune cells after they've served their purpose. Option A describes necrosis, which is uncontrolled cell death due to injury. Option C relates to the pathological cell changes in cancer. Option D describes muscle fiber damage, not cell death through apoptosis.

questions.text: The event at metaphase-I which increases the genetic diversity is the:
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: Formation of bivalents at the equator
options.explanation: Bivalents are formed earlier during prophase-I when homologous chromosomes pair up. While critical for meiosis, their formation itself doesn't increase genetic diversity.

options.text: Contraction of the spindle fibres at both poles of the cell
options.explanation: Spindle fibers are involved in the separation of chromosomes but do not directly contribute to genetic diversity during metaphase-I.

options.text: Random orientation of homologous chromosomes at the equator
options.explanation: The random orientation of homologous chromosome pairs increases genetic diversity by creating different combinations of chromosomes that will be segregated into the daughter cells.

options.text: Dissociation of synaptonemal complex between homologous chromosomes
options.explanation: The synaptonemal complex dissociates during the transition from prophase-I to metaphase-I, but this process is not directly responsible for increasing genetic diversity.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is the random orientation of homologous chromosomes at the equator during metaphase-I. This process, known as independent assortment, results in different combinations of maternal and paternal chromosomes being distributed to the gametes, thus increasing genetic diversity. The formation of bivalents and the dissociation of the synaptonemal complex are important processes in meiosis but do not directly contribute to genetic diversity. The contraction of spindle fibers facilitates chromosome movement but does not affect genetic variation.

questions.text: Below are three processes that occur in the cells of living organisms.I. ApoptosisII. MitosisIII. MeiosisWhich of these process(es) is/ are important in determining the body plan of an organism?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Apoptosis is crucial for shaping the body by removing unnecessary or damaged cells, but it alone does not establish the overall body plan.
Mitosis is important for growth and tissue repair, contributing to the body plan, but it is not the only process involved.

Apoptosis and mitosis both play roles in shaping the organism hence this option is correct.Apoptosis is programmed cell death.It is essential for shaping the body plan during embryonic develMitosis increases the number of cells in the developing embryo.These cells then differentiate to form tissues and organs in the correct places.Thus, mitosis helps establish and maintain the body structure and pattern.

Mitosis contributes to the development and structure of the organism by allowing cells to proliferate, while meiosis is crucial for the formation of gametes, enabling genetic diversity which influences the overall body plan.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is II and I. Mitosis plays a significant role in the growth and maintenance of tissues, which shapes the body plan by ensuring cells are available for these processes. ultimately influencing the body plan. Apoptosis, important for removing unwanted cells and shaping certain structures,ultimately influencing the body plan. Meiosis, although primarily involved in the formation of gametes, contributes to the genetic variation necessary for evolutionary adaptations and diversity,

questions.text: The process that occurs during prophase of the mitotic cell cycle in an animal cell is the:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: DNA replication occurs during the S phase of the cell cycle, not during prophase. During prophase, the cell is preparing for mitosis, not replicating DNA.
During prophase, centrioles move to opposite poles of the cell, helping to organize the mitotic spindle. This is a correct description of an event during prophase.
Centromeres divide during anaphase, not during prophase. In prophase, chromosomes condense and become visible, but centromeres are not yet divided.
Chromatids are formed during the S phase when DNA is replicated. In prophase, chromosomes, which consist of two sister chromatids, become visible and condense.

questions.explanation: During prophase of the mitotic cell cycle, one of the key processes is the separation of centrioles, which move to opposite poles of the cell and begin forming the spindle apparatus necessary for chromosomal separation. This is a critical step in preparing for metaphase and eventual cell division. The other options describe events that occur at different stages of the cell cycle: DNA replication occurs during the S phase, centromeres divide during anaphase, and chromatids are formed as a result of DNA replication earlier in the cell cycle.

questions.text: Given is the base sequence of a primer used in the polymerase chain reaction.ATGACAAATCGThe base sequence of a DNA fragment to which this primer would bind is
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This option incorrectly assumes that uracil (U) is a component of DNA, which is not the case.
This option incorrectly uses uracil (U) in a DNA sequence, which is incorrect. DNA uses thymine (T) instead.
This option correctly uses thymine (T) in place of uracil (U) and follows the base pairing rules correctly.
This option also incorrectly assumes that uracil (U) is a component of DNA.

questions.explanation: The sequence provided is a DNA primer, which means it is composed of DNA bases: adenine (A), thymine (T), cytosine (C), and guanine (G). DNA does not contain uracil (U), which is found in RNA. The correct complementary base pairing for the sequence ATGACAAATCG would be TACTGTTTAGC, where adenine pairs with thymine, and cytosine pairs with guanine. Therefore, the correct answer is Option C. Options A, B, and D incorrectly use uracil, which is not present in DNA.

questions.text: The given reaction is used to clone genes in the laboratory.mRNA →cDNA(Note: mRNA=messenger ribonucleic acid; cDNA=complementary deoxyribonucleic acid)Enzyme X for this reaction is:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Endonucleases are enzymes that cut DNA strands at specific sequences. They are not involved in synthesizing cDNA from mRNA.
Taq polymerase is an enzyme used to amplify DNA sequences during PCR (Polymerase Chain Reaction). It does not synthesize cDNA from mRNA.
DNA polymerase synthesizes DNA from a DNA template, not from an RNA template.
Reverse transcriptase is the enzyme that synthesizes complementary DNA (cDNA) from an RNA template, such as mRNA. This process is essential for cloning genes in the laboratory.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is reverse transcriptase. This enzyme is specifically used to convert messenger RNA (mRNA) into complementary DNA (cDNA), which is a crucial step in cloning genes. The other enzymes listed have different functions: endonucleases are involved in cutting DNA strands, Taq polymerase is used in PCR to amplify DNA, and DNA polymerase synthesizes DNA from a DNA template, not an RNA template.

questions.text: The role of a genetic counsellor is to inform parents about the:
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: Risks of appearance of disorders in the baby
options.explanation: Genetic counsellors evaluate the risk of inherited disorders in a baby, helping parents understand potential genetic conditions.

options.text: Gender of the baby using the ultrasound technique
options.explanation: Determining the gender of a baby is typically done by an ultrasound technician, not a genetic counsellor.

options.text: Benefits of family planning for young parents
options.explanation: Family planning education is generally provided by healthcare providers specializing in reproductive health, not genetic counsellors.

options.text: Nutritional requirements of a mother and her baby
options.explanation: Nutritional guidance is usually given by dietitians or healthcare providers, not genetic counsellors.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option A. The role of a genetic counsellor is to assess and communicate the risks of genetic disorders in a baby, providing vital information to parents about potential genetic conditions. Option B is incorrect because determining the baby's gender is not typically within the scope of genetic counselling; it is done through ultrasound. Option C is incorrect as family planning advice is not the primary focus of genetic counsellors. Option D is incorrect because nutritional counselling is usually the responsibility of dietitians or healthcare providers, not genetic counsellors.

questions.text: Cardiac muscles of frog are identified under the microscope by the presence of
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This is the correct answer. Cardiac muscles are characterized by their striations and branched, interconnected fibers.
This is incorrect. While striations are present, cardiac muscle fibers are branched, not unbranched.
This is incorrect. Cardiac muscle cells typically have a single nucleus and branched fibers.
This is incorrect. While the fibers are branched, cardiac muscle cells usually have a single nucleus, not multiple nuclei.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option A: 'striations and branched fibres.' Cardiac muscle tissue is distinct for its striated appearance and branched fibers, which allow for strong and synchronized contractions. Unlike skeletal muscle, which has striations with unbranched and multinucleated fibers, cardiac muscle fibers are typically branched and contain a single nucleus per cell. Therefore, Options B, C, and D are incorrect due to their inaccurate descriptions of cardiac muscle characteristics.

questions.text: For the preparation of the temporary slide of the smooth muscles of frog, the sample is taken from the dissected organs.The organ suitable for the selection of smooth muscles is
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The skin primarily contains epithelial and connective tissues, not smooth muscle.
The heart is composed of cardiac muscle, which is distinct from smooth muscle.
Forelimbs contain skeletal muscle, which is different from smooth muscle.
The urinary bladder is composed of smooth muscle, which allows it to expand and contract.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is the urinary bladder. Smooth muscle is found in the walls of hollow organs such as the urinary bladder, where it helps control the flow of urine. The other options do not primarily consist of smooth muscle: the skin contains mostly epithelial and connective tissue, the heart is made of cardiac muscle, and the forelimb contains skeletal muscle.

questions.text: In the human skeleton, fibula proximally articulates with the
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The fibula does articulate with the tibia, but it is at the proximal end, not just the lateral side.
The distal end of the femur articulates with the tibia, not the fibula.
The fibula is not located on the anterior side of the tibia; it is more lateral.
The fibula articulates with the proximal end of the tibia, specifically at the lateral aspect.

questions.explanation: The fibula is a bone located in the lower leg, running parallel to the tibia. It primarily serves as a site for muscle attachment rather than significant weight-bearing. Proximally, the fibula articulates with the tibia at the lateral side, forming part of the knee joint, but it does not articulate with the femur. The femur is the thigh bone, and its distal end forms the knee joint with the tibia, not the fibula.

questions.text: Ankle joint is formed by the articulation of
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: Distal end of tibia and carpal bones
options.explanation: The carpal bones are part of the wrist, not the ankle. Hence, this combination does not form the ankle joint.

options.text: Distal end of fibula and tarsal bones
options.explanation: While the fibula is involved in forming the ankle joint, it is the distal end that articulates with the tarsal bones, specifically the talus.

options.text: Proximal end of tibia and carpal bones
options.explanation: The proximal end of the tibia is part of the knee joint, not the ankle, and the carpal bones belong to the wrist.

options.text: Proximal end of fibula and tarsal bones
options.explanation: The proximal end of the fibula is located near the knee, and is not involved in forming the ankle joint.

questions.explanation: The ankle joint, also known as the talocrural joint, is formed by the articulation of the distal ends of the tibia and fibula with the tarsal bones, specifically the talus. This allows for the complex movements required for walking and running. Option B is correct because it references the distal end of the fibula and tarsal bones, which accurately describes the anatomical structure of the ankle joint. The other options incorrectly refer to carpal bones, which are part of the wrist, or the proximal ends of the bones, which are not involved in the ankle joint.

questions.text: In horses, black colouring (B) is dominant and chestnut colouring (b) is recessive. A homozygous black horse crosses with a chestnut horse.The percentage of offspring that are expected to be chestnut is:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: In this genetic cross, a homozygous black horse (BB) is crossed with a chestnut horse (bb). The offspring will all inherit one allele from each parent. Since the black horse can only pass on a 'B' allele and the chestnut horse can only pass on a 'b' allele, all offspring will be heterozygous (Bb) and exhibit black colouring because black (B) is dominant over chestnut (b). Therefore, 0% of the offspring will be chestnut.
This would be possible if both horses were heterozygous dominant (black phenotype).
This would be possible if the phenotypically black horse were a heterozygote.
Chestnut offspring are not possible with the given mating individuals.

questions.explanation: In this genetic cross, a homozygous black horse (BB) is crossed with a chestnut horse (bb). The offspring will all inherit one allele from each parent. Since the black horse can only pass on a 'B' allele and the chestnut horse can only pass on a 'b' allele, all offspring will be heterozygous (Bb) and exhibit black colouring because black (B) is dominant over chestnut (b). Therefore, 0% of the offspring will be chestnut.

questions.text: In dogs, black coat colour (B) is dominant over yellow coat colour (b), and straight fur (C) is dominant over curly fur (c).In a cross between two BbCc parents, the predicted fraction of offspring with black coat colour and straight fur would be:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Because the traits are independently assorted, and the probability of a black coat (B) is 3/4 and the probability of straight fur (C) is 3/4. Therefore, the probability of both occurring together is (3/4) * (3/4) = 9/16 not 3/4.

Because the traits are independently assorted, and the probability of a black coat (B) is 3/4 and the probability of straight fur (C) is 3/4. Therefore, the probability of both occurring together is (3/4) * (3/4) = 9/16 not 1/4.

The predicted fraction of offspring with black coat color and straight fur in a cross between two BbCc parents is 9/16. This is because the traits are independently assorted, and the probability of a black coat (B) is 3/4 and the probability of straight fur (C) is 3/4. Therefore, the probability of both occurring together is (3/4) * (3/4) = 9/16.

Because the traits are independently assorted, and the probability of a black coat (B) is 3/4 and the probability of straight fur (C) is 3/4. Therefore, the probability of both occurring together is (3/4) * (3/4) = 9/16 not 1/16.

questions.explanation: The predicted fraction of offspring with black coat color and straight fur in a cross between two BbCc parents is 9/16. This is because the traits are independently assorted, and the probability of a black coat (B) is 3/4 and the probability of straight fur (C) is 3/4. Therefore, the probability of both occurring together is (3/4) * (3/4) = 9/16. So option C is correct. The rest of the options give wrong probabilities.

questions.text: The hormone produced by the labelled structure X is
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Estrogen is primarily produced by the ovaries, not by the structure X which is corpus luteum and responsible for producing progesterone
Progesterone is mainly produced by the corpus luteum in the ovaries which is structure X.
The pituitary gland is a structure that releases hormones, but it is not a hormone itself.
Luteinising hormone (LH) is produced by the anterior pituitary gland not Structure X.

questions.explanation: The structure labelled X is the corpus luteum, which is responsible for producing progesterone. Progesterone levels rise after ovulation, thickening the uterine lining to prepare for a potential pregnancy. Estrogen and progesterone are produced by the ovaries, not by the pituitary gland. The option 'pituitary gland' refers to the structure itself, not a hormone.

questions.text: In the cortical region of the mammalian ovary, all of the following are observed under the microscope EXCEPT the
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Oocytes are developing egg cells that are indeed found in the cortical region of the ovary.

Blood vessels are present throughout the ovary, While small capillaries and vessels do supply the growing follicles (Theca interna layer is highly vascularized), the major arteries and veins are generally found in the medulla. Thus this option is correct.
The corpus luteum (formed from the ruptured follicle after ovulation) is located in the cortex.
Primary follicles are found within the cortical region of the ovary, in contrast to the question's assertion.

questions.explanation: The cortical region of the mammalian ovary contains structures such as oocytes and primary follicles. The corpus luteum is located in the cortex. Blood vessels permeate the ovary to supply nutrients and remove waste. While small capillaries and vessels do supply the growing follicles (the theca interna layer is highly vascularized), the major arteries and veins are generally found in the medulla. Thus, blood vessels is the most suitable answer.

questions.text: The given diagram shows the transverse section of a leaf.The plant having such leaves will be MAINLY found in a:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Plants in ponds often have broad, flat leaves to float and are adapted to aquatic environments, not extreme cold.
River plants typically have flexible, strong stems to withstand flowing water, which is different from adaptations for cold climates.
Desert plants are adapted to conserve water with succulent leaves or spines, unlike the adaptations needed for cold regions.
Plants in polar regions have adaptations like small, thick leaves to minimize heat loss and survive extreme cold, which matches the leaf structure shown.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option D: polar region.The transverse section of the leaf likely shows adaptations like thick cuticles or reduced surface area, which are typical for plants in polar regions to conserve heat and protect against freezing temperatures.

questions.text: The animal that is CORRECTLY categorised on the basis of the production of metabolic heatby its body is
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Whales are endothermic not exothermic, meaning they generate their own body heat and maintain a stable internal temperature regardless of the surrounding environment.
Mice are not exothermic. They are endothermic, meaning they generate their own body heat and maintain a relatively constant internal body temperature, regardless of the external temperature.
Sparrows are endothermic, meaning they generate their own body heat and maintain a relatively constant internal body temperature, regardless of the external temperature.

Crocodiles are exothermic, also known as "cold-blooded". This means they rely on external sources like the sun to regulate their body temperature, rather than generating heat internally like mammals and birds.

questions.explanation: Sparrows are endothermic, meaning they generate their own body heat and maintain a relatively constant internal body temperature, regardless of the external temperature. So option C is correct. All other options are wrongly matched.
hints: metabolic heat by its body is

questions.text: Bony fish have some physiological adaptations which enable them to live in sea water.The adaptation in glomerular filtration rate and the function of the salt secretory cells in the gills of these fish is
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Bony fish have adapted to sea water by excreting salts and decreasing not increasing their glomerular filtration rate.
This option is correct because bony fish have adapted to seawater by decreasing their glomerular filtration rate to conserve water and using specialized cells in their gills to excrete excess salt.
Bony fish have adapted to sea water by decreasing GFR but excreting salts not absorbing.
Absorbing salts and increasing GFR are not the adptations of bony fish to sea water.

questions.explanation: Bony fish have adapted to seawater by decreasing their glomerular filtration rate to conserve water and using specialized cells in their gills to excrete excess salt. So option B is correct. All other options do not give the correct set of adaptations.
